code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Each predicate is mapped to a short phrase; the resulting phrases are
    concatenated with commas and an “and” before the final phrase.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a natural‑language template.
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "{subject} features {object} architectural style",
        "buildingStartDate": "{subject} started construction in {object}",
        "completionDate": "{subject} was completed in {object}",
        "floorCount": "{subject} has {object} floors",
        "location": "{subject} is located at {object}",
        "cost": "the cost of {subject} is {object}",
        "floorArea": "{subject} has a floor area of {object} square metres",
        "architect": "{subject} was designed by {object}",
        "owner": "{subject} is owned by {object}",
        "formerName": "{subject} was formerly known as {object}",
        "height": "{subject} stands {object} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "{subject} is a {object}",
        "developer": "{subject} was developed by {object}",
        "tenant": "{subject} has tenant {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "country": "{subject} is in {object}",
        "currentTenants": "{subject} currently houses {object}",
        "address": "{subject} is located at {object}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{subject} was inaugurated on {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
        "bedCount": "{subject} has {object} beds",
        "region": "{subject} is in the {object} region",
        "state": "{subject} is in {object}",
        "website": "{subject}'s website is {object}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{subject} was constructed in {object}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": (
            "{subject} has NRHP reference number {object}"
        ),
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": (
            "{subject} was added to the NRHP on {object}"
        ),
        "significantBuilding": "{subject} is known for the building {object}",
        "governingBody": "{subject} is governed by {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "chancellor": "{subject}'s chancellor is {object}",
        "governmentType": "{subject} has a government type of {object}",
        "capital": "{subject}'s capital is {object}",
        "language": "{subject} speaks {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subject}'s leader title is {object}",
        "currency": "{subject}'s currency is {object}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{subject} includes ethnic group {object}",
        "origin": "{subject} originates from {object}",
        "significantProject": "{subject} is known for the project {object}",
        "foundationPlace": "{subject} was founded in {object}",
        "demonym": "{subject}'s demonym is {object}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{subject} has {object} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "{subject}'s key person is {object}",
        "architecture": "{subject} features architecture: {object}",
        "postalCode": "{subject}'s postal code is {object}",
    }

    phrases = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate, None)
        if tmpl:
            phrase = tmpl.format(subject=triple.subject, object=triple.object)
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering
            phrase = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
        phrases.append(phrase)

    if not phrases:
        return ""

    # Join phrases into a single sentence
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        sentence = phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
